topic: Understanding Beverage Grade CO₂: What It Takes to Meet the Standard
description: A deep dive into what the ISBT beverage grade specification actually requires, why most CO₂ sources struggle to meet it, and what separates technical grade from beverage grade purification.

topic: Carbon Capture from Renewable Natural Gas: An Untapped CO₂ Source
description: RNG upgraders produce a concentrated CO₂ stream as a byproduct that is typically vented. Here is why capturing and purifying that CO₂ is both economically viable and critical for domestic supply.

topic: The 2022 CO₂ Shortage: What It Exposed About America's Supply Chain
description: The 2022 CO₂ shortage left beverage producers scrambling and food processors cutting output. A breakdown of what went wrong, why it keeps happening, and what needs to change.

topic: Why Domestic CO₂ Supply Matters More Than Ever
description: The U.S. depends on a handful of large centralized CO₂ sources. Distributed domestic production from biogenic sources can reduce volatility and strengthen the supply chain for every buyer.

topic: How On-Site CO₂ Capture Works: From Waste Gas to Usable Product
description: A practical walkthrough of how on-site carbon capture systems take raw waste gas and turn it into purified, usable CO₂ product ready for industrial and beverage applications.

topic: 45Q Tax Credits and Carbon Capture Economics: What Project Developers Need to Know
description: The 45Q tax credit is a significant driver for carbon capture projects. A clear explanation of utilization vs. storage credits, qualification thresholds, and how they affect project economics.

topic: Biogenic CO₂ vs. Fossil Derived CO₂: Why the Source Matters
description: Not all CO₂ is created equal from a sustainability perspective. Understanding the difference between biogenic and fossil derived CO₂ and why buyers increasingly care about the distinction.

topic: Food Grade vs. Beverage Grade CO₂: Understanding the Specifications
description: The terms food grade and beverage grade are often used interchangeably, but they are not the same. A clear breakdown of the different specification tiers and what applications each serves.

topic: Cryogenic Purification vs. Chemical Absorption: Choosing the Right CO₂ Cleanup Technology
description: Two dominant approaches to CO₂ purification serve different use cases. A technical comparison of cryogenic distillation and chemical absorption, including cost, purity, and operational tradeoffs.

topic: The Business Case for Distributed Carbon Capture
description: Large centralized plants dominate CO₂ supply today. Why smaller, distributed capture projects co-located at RNG facilities and digesters can deliver better economics and supply resilience.

topic: CO₂ in Agriculture and Greenhouse Applications: A Growing Market
description: CO₂ enrichment in greenhouses can boost crop yields 20 to 30 percent. As controlled environment agriculture scales, demand for reliable, affordable CO₂ supply is growing fast.

topic: Industrial CO₂ Market Outlook: Supply, Demand, and the Path Forward
description: An analysis of the current U.S. industrial CO₂ market including supply concentration, demand growth across sectors, pricing trends, and where new supply will need to come from.
